req.title: Overdraft protection
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-012 defines three behaviorally distinct partitions: (1) debit exceeds checking balance but linked savings has sufficient funds -> overdraft draw + fee (positive/EP); (2) debit exceeds checking balance and linked savings also insufficient -> decline (negative/NEG); (3) debit equals exactly checking balance + linked savings balance -> boundary where transaction succeeds by exactly consuming all savings (boundary/BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'overdraft triggered' to 'fee assessed' to ensure the system correctly records the fee as a discrete state change (STC). A fifth test uses a decision table to cover the combination where checking balance is zero and savings covers exactly the debit (DT). Each test resets account state via fixtures to guarantee idempotency.

tests.name: test_overdraft_draw_from_linked_savings_success
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with available_balance=200.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and accounts are linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=150.00 against the checking account
expected: checking_balance remains 0.00 or negative only by the shortfall drawn from savings
savings debited by the overdraft shortfall (100.00)
overdraft fee assessed and present in response
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_overdraft_draw_from_linked_savings_success(api_client, reset_accounts):
    checking_id, savings_id = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=200.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": checking_id,
        "debit_amount": 150.00
    }
    response = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body["checking_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(100.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    assert body["savings_debited"] == pytest.approx(100.00, abs=1e-2)

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with available_balance=30.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and accounts are linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=100.00 against the checking account
expected: HTTP 422 or 402 returned indicating insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-012: error pathway - declined when linked balance insufficient
R-FIN-012: linked savings balance < shortfall amount
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient(api_client, reset_accounts):
    checking_id, savings_id = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=10.00,
        savings_balance=30.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": checking_id,
        "debit_amount": 100.00
    }
    response = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "declined"
    assert body["checking_balance"] == pytest.approx(10.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(30.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body.get("overdraft_fee", 0) == 0

tests.name: test_overdraft_boundary_debit_equals_combined_balance
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with available_balance=50.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and accounts are linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=100.00, exactly equal to checking_balance + savings_balance
expected: Transaction approved at the exact combined balance limit
trace: R-FIN-012: boundary where debit == checking + savings (last valid point)
R-FIN-012: linked savings debited to zero
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_overdraft_boundary_debit_equals_combined_balance(api_client, reset_accounts):
    checking_id, savings_id = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=50.00,
        savings_balance=50.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": checking_id,
        "debit_amount": 100.00
    }
    response = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body["checking_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transition_fee_recorded
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with available_balance=100.00
No prior overdraft fees exist on the account
expected: First call returns HTTP 200 with status approved
State transitions: PENDING -> OVERDRAFT_TRIGGERED -> FEE_ASSESSED -> COMPLETED
Transaction history contains exactly one overdraft fee entry
Fee entry has a positive amount and correct type
trace: R-FIN-012: fee assessed as discrete post-draw state
R-FIN-012: triggered -> completed state transition traceable in ledger
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_overdraft_state_transition_fee_recorded(api_client, reset_accounts):
    checking_id, savings_id = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=20.00,
        savings_balance=100.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    payload = {"checking_account_id": checking_id, "debit_amount": 70.00}
    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "approved"

    hist = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{checking_id}/transactions")
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    txns = hist.json()["transactions"]
    fee_entries = [t for t in txns if t.get("type") == "overdraft_fee"]
    assert len(fee_entries) == 1
    assert fee_entries[0]["amount"] > 0
    assert fee_entries[0]["state"] == "COMPLETED"

tests.name: test_overdraft_decision_table_zero_checking_savings_covers_debit
preconditions: Linked savings account exists with available_balance=75.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and accounts are linked
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=75.00 (checking is zero, full amount drawn from savings)
trace: R-FIN-012: DT rule - checking=0, savings>=debit -> approve and draw fully from savings
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_overdraft_decision_table_zero_checking_savings_covers_debit(api_client, reset_accounts):
    checking_id, savings_id = reset_accounts(
        checking_balance=0.00,
        savings_balance=75.00,
        overdraft_linked=True
    )
    payload = {
        "checking_account_id": checking_id,
        "debit_amount": 75.00
    }
    response = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload)
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body["checking_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["savings_balance"] == pytest.approx(0.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["savings_debited"] == pytest.approx(75.00, abs=1e-2)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
